response.justification: Maria Rodriguez is an experienced full-stack web and backend engineer with strong TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang skills, but her C++ exposure is limited to approximately six months at National Instruments in 2014–2015 and a bachelor thesis project — nowhere near the depth required for a senior C++ role. She has no industrial automation background, no experience with IEC 62443 or Secure Development Lifecycle practices, and no exposure to the industrial protocols (Modbus, OPC UA, MQTT, IEC 61850) central to this position. Her career trajectory points firmly toward web-product and cloud-native development rather than the high-performance systems and embedded/industrial domain this role demands.
strengths: Solid event-driven and distributed-systems experience (GISCollective, Sociomantic micro-services at millions of RPS)
Strong software-craftsmanship culture: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, mentoring, and public knowledge-sharing
Some C++ touchpoint at National Instruments writing components for an engineering IDE, showing baseline familiarity with the language
concerns: C++ is a marginal and dated portion of the resume (~6 months, 2014–2015); the role requires 8–12 years of deep C++/STL/OOP expertise
Zero industrial automation, IEC 62443, SDL, or industrial-protocol experience (Modbus, OPC UA, IEC 61850, DNP 3.0, MQTT)
Entire career is web/TypeScript/DLang-centric with no systems-level networking, multi-threading, or embedded development at meaningful scale
